# Caugia — GTM Diagnostics & Operating System for B2B SaaS

> Caugia is a GTM diagnostics company that builds deterministic scoring engines for B2B SaaS revenue systems. The core products are the GTM Intelligence Report (45-page board-level diagnostic) and GRIP OS (constraint-driven GTM execution system with AI advisor).

## What Caugia Does

Caugia evaluates Go-to-Market systems across four structural dimensions using the GRIP Framework (Guidance, Resources, Implementation, Performance). The diagnostic identifies the primary structural constraint in a revenue system, quantifies its impact, and sequences the interventions required to resolve it.

## Products

### GTM Intelligence Report
One-time board-level diagnostic. 265 questions across 12 GTM pillars, scored by 72 deterministic engines. Delivers a 45-page report with constraint analysis, revenue leakage quantification, growth archetype identification, and a 90-day execution plan. Delivered within 1 hour of submission.

**Pricing:**
- EUR: €2,495
- USD: $2,995
- GBP: £2,295

### GRIP OS Core
Constraint governance dashboard with action cascades, gates, drift detection, and team collaboration.

**Pricing:**
- EUR: €799/month
- USD: $895/month
- GBP: £695/month

### GRIP OS Full
Everything in Core plus GRIP Brain (Sophie) AI advisor, voice interface, 340+ connectors, Board Deck generator, and custom benchmarks.

**Pricing:**
- EUR: €1,495/month
- USD: $1,695/month
- GBP: £1,295/month

### GRIP Brain (Sophie)
AI advisor powered by Claude that has access to all diagnostic data, scores, benchmarks, and context for your workspace. Ask anything about your GTM system via text or voice. Sophie provides data-backed answers grounded in your actual metrics and GRIP diagnostic results.

### Free GTM Score Quiz
10 questions. 2 minutes. Instant directional signal on GTM maturity across the GRIP framework. No email required, no paywall. Provides a score and high-level breakdown by dimension.

## The GRIP Framework — 12 Pillars in Detail

### Guidance (G)

#### 1. GTM Strategy & Leadership
Evaluates strategic clarity, ICP definition, positioning coherence, and leadership alignment. Scores the presence of a documented GTM strategy with measurable objectives. Key signals: strategy documentation, ICP specificity, TAM/SAM methodology, leadership consensus on priorities.

#### 2. Market Intelligence
Assesses competitive awareness, market sizing methodology, buyer research depth, and win/loss analysis maturity. Determines whether market data informs GTM decisions. Key signals: competitive tracking cadence, buyer persona validation, win/loss review process.

#### 3. Product Marketing
Measures messaging-market fit, positioning differentiation, content production, and launch readiness. Evaluates whether product marketing connects product value to buyer needs. Key signals: messaging testing, competitive positioning, launch playbooks, content-to-pipeline attribution.

### Resources (R)

#### 4. Pricing & Packaging
Evaluates pricing model alignment with value delivery, packaging architecture, discount governance, and expansion pricing. Identifies revenue leakage from pricing misalignment. Key signals: price elasticity data, discount approval workflows, packaging tier logic, expansion triggers.

#### 5. Product Readiness
Assesses product-market fit signals, onboarding friction, time-to-value, and feature adoption. Determines whether the product supports the GTM motion. Key signals: activation rate, time-to-value, feature adoption distribution, NPS/CSAT trends.

#### 6. Enablement
Measures sales and CS enablement maturity, training cadence, content accessibility, and ramp time. Evaluates whether teams have the tools and knowledge to execute. Key signals: rep ramp time, content utilization, training completion rates, enablement satisfaction.

### Implementation (I)

#### 7. Demand Generation
Evaluates pipeline generation efficiency, channel mix, conversion rates, CAC by channel, and attribution maturity. Identifies demand generation bottlenecks. Key signals: pipeline coverage ratio, CAC by channel, MQL-to-SQL conversion, attribution model sophistication.

#### 8. Sales Execution
Assesses sales process adherence, win rates, deal velocity, forecast accuracy, and rep productivity. Scores execution discipline and deal management. Key signals: stage-by-stage conversion, average deal cycle, forecast variance, quota attainment distribution.

#### 9. Revenue Operations & Systems
Measures RevOps maturity, system integration depth, data hygiene, automation coverage, and reporting infrastructure. Evaluates operational backbone strength. Key signals: CRM data completeness, integration count, automation coverage, reporting latency.

### Performance (P)

#### 10. Customer Success & Expansion
Evaluates NRR, churn drivers, expansion motion, health scoring, and CS capacity. Identifies post-sale revenue risk and expansion opportunity. Key signals: gross/net retention, churn reason taxonomy, expansion revenue %, health score methodology.

#### 11. Data & Insights
Assesses data infrastructure, metric definitions, dashboard coverage, and data-driven decision maturity. Determines whether the organization can measure what matters. Key signals: metric definition consistency, dashboard coverage, data freshness, insight-to-action latency.

#### 12. Alignment & Governance
Measures cross-functional alignment, meeting cadence, shared metrics, handoff quality, and accountability structures. Identifies coordination failures that create drag. Key signals: shared KPI adoption, handoff SLAs, cross-functional review cadence, accountability documentation.

## Scoring Methodology

GRIP OS uses 72 deterministic scoring engines. Each engine takes structured inputs and produces a score — same inputs always produce the same outputs. There is no AI in the scoring: it is rule-based and transparent.

The system identifies:
- **Primary Constraint**: The single pillar most limiting revenue growth
- **Constraint Sequence**: The optimal order of interventions for maximum impact
- **Revenue Leakage**: Estimated ARR gap between potential and actual performance (typically 15-30%)
- **Growth Archetypes**: 30+ structural patterns describing specific GTM imbalances

Scoring tiers:
- 0-39: Critical — Structural failure requiring immediate intervention
- 40-59: Weak — Significant gaps limiting system performance
- 60-74: Developing — Foundations present but optimization needed
- 75-89: Strong — Well-functioning with refinement opportunities
- 90-100: Elite — Best-in-class execution

## Core Concepts

- **Revenue Architecture:** The structural design of how a company converts market opportunity into recurring revenue.
- **Revenue Leakage:** The gap between the revenue a GTM system could produce and what it actually produces, typically 15-30% of potential ARR.
- **Constraint Sequence:** The order in which interventions should be applied to produce maximum system improvement with minimum investment.
- **Growth Archetypes:** Structural patterns (30+) that describe specific GTM imbalances and their consequences.
- **Constraint Engine:** The deterministic scoring system that evaluates all 12 pillars and identifies the binding constraint.
- **Action Cascades:** Sequenced interventions that flow from constraint identification through resolution.
- **Drift Detection:** Continuous monitoring that identifies when execution deviates from the prescribed constraint resolution path.

## Languages Available

Caugia is fully localized in four languages.

- **English (default, x-default):** https://www.caugia.com/
- **French:** https://www.caugia.com/fr/ (full parity with English)
- **German:** https://www.caugia.com/de/ (core 15 pages)
- **Spanish (Castilian, es-ES):** https://www.caugia.com/es/ (full parity with French: 27 root pages + 28 intelligence articles)

Localized URL pattern: prepend the locale prefix to any English path.
- EN: https://www.caugia.com/intelligence/revenue-leakage-saas.html
- FR: https://www.caugia.com/fr/intelligence/revenue-leakage-saas.html
- ES: https://www.caugia.com/es/intelligence/revenue-leakage-saas.html

GRIP OS (product) locale URLs:
- EN: https://os.caugia.com/demo
- FR: https://os.caugia.com/fr/demo
- DE: https://os.caugia.com/de/demo
- ES: https://os.caugia.com/es/demo

Hreflang alternates are declared in each page's head and in sitemap.xml.
